source_jobs.title: Senior .Net Engineer - Full Stack
source_jobs.description:
As a remote Senior .NET Software Engineer at TeamDynamix, you'll lend your software craftsmanship to our work-management software. We empower customers to do more with fewer resources as they track and manage much of their daily work within the tools created and maintained by you.

We're a multi-tenant SaaS company running on Azure competing with companies more than 10 times our size and we're able to be successful because we hire only the best and brightest. Our small Work Management engineering team of 20 (and growing!) is made up of two smaller full-stack engineering teams plus a quality assurance team to conduct this mission.

In our remote-first environment, every engineer can contribute ideas that get heard and implemented. We believe that technical decisions are best made when we have input from those who are closest to the problem and encourage engineers to think critically about how best to design solutions that satisfy the mission of the team.



Our Tech Stack

Front End

 - ASP.NET MVC & WebForms

 - Single Page Applications (SPA) using modern frameworks like Angular and Vue

 - HTML/CSS/JavaScript

 - Bootstrap

 - JQuery/AJAX

Back End

 - Microservices written in .NET 8 running on Kubernetes

 - RESTful WebAPI

 - Current .NET 4.8 Full Framework Web Applications

 - Redis Cache

 - SQL Server

 - Entity Framework

 - SaaS solution hosted in Microsoft Azure

Tooling

 - Visual Studio 2026 & VS Code

 - Azure DevOps for Source Control and CI/CD



About You

Senior .NET Software Engineers have a heavy focus on individual contributions to initiatives and are also expected to manage large initiatives from start to finish. In addition to working well with others and being able to plan and execute, you’ll need the following technical skills:

 - 5+ years of software development experience.

 - .NET experience.

 - JavaScript, HTML, and CSS.

 - Experience with SQL Server, T-SQL, stored procedures, and basic database design. We’d prefer a full-stack developer who can work from database to front-end, but you don’t necessarily have to be an SQL guru.

 - If you believe you would add value but don't meet all the criteria we've laid out, we'd still love to hear from you.



What We Offer

 - Competitive compensation that reflects employees’ skills and contributions.

 - Comprehensive health insurance plans.

 - Wellness programs that promote physical and mental well-being.

 - Traditional and Roth 401(k) savings options with company match.

 - Remote-first with flexible working hours.

 - Prioritized work-life balance, with 15 observed days off in addition to PTO.

 - Family-friendly policies including paid parental leave.

 - Paid life insurance, short-term, and long-term disability insurance.

 - Regular acknowledgement of achievements with recognition programs.

 - Counseling services and resources for personal and professional challenges.

 - Transparent communication about company goals, strategy, and performance.

 - Regular feedback sessions and performance reviews.

 - Anti-bias and anti-harassment training programs.

 - Trust and autonomy in decision-making within your role.

 - Work environment that encourages you to be your authentic self.

 - Flat management structure with approachable leaders who want to hear your feedback.

 - Backed by two private equity firms that invest in high-growth private companies across North America.
   
   

Diversity

At TeamDynamix, our goal is to create a remote environment which celebrates the differences that make us, us. We strive to create an inclusive and equitable space, but we realize we don’t always get it right. We’re continually working together to create mutual respect and opportunities to learn from one another and we encourage people of color, women, individuals with disabilities and LGBTQIA+ individuals to apply.

We’re looking for team members located in the U.S. regardless of race, ethnicity, age, national origin, marital status, parental status, veteran status,

source_jobs.title: Managing Consultant, Technical Services
source_jobs.description:
TeamDynamix is seeking a highly technical, solutions-oriented, and customer-focused Managing Technical Consultant to join our growing Professional Services team. In this role, you will partner directly with customers to design and implement technical solutions, build integrations between TeamDynamix and client systems, and help solve complex business challenges through automation and technology. This remote opportunity is ideal for candidates with strong software engineering experience, a consultative mindset, and the ability to balance technical depth with exceptional client partnership.

 

About the Role

 - Partner directly with customers to provide technical expertise and help resolve complex implementation and integration challenges.

 - Design, build, configure, and maintain integrations between TeamDynamix solutions and client systems.

 - Identify opportunities for automation and implement technology solutions that improve efficiency and solve business problems.

 - Gather and document technical requirements, working closely with customers to translate business needs into scalable solutions.

 - Troubleshoot technical issues and drive timely resolution to ensure high customer satisfaction and minimize disruption.

 - Serve as a technical subject matter expert and provide guidance to other members of the Professional Services team.

 - Collaborate cross-functionally with Professional Services leadership to influence the strategic direction and roadmap of the division.

 - Partner closely with Sales to improve solution scoping and help create more accurate Statements of Work (SOWs).

 - Recommend and implement process improvements that maximize team resources and operational effectiveness.

 - Deliver high-quality consulting services and maintain strong, trusted relationships with customers.

 - Provide regular project updates and communicate effectively with internal stakeholders and clients.

 

About You

 - Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science or a related technical field.

 - 5+ years of experience in software engineering, database report writing, or technical solution development.

 - 7–10+ years of experience within computer technology, technical services, or related fields.

 - Experience working directly with customers in a technical consulting or support capacity.

 - Ability to define business requirements and translate them into creative, scalable technical solutions.

 - Strong troubleshooting and problem-solving skills, with the ability to manage highly complex issues independently.

 - Excellent communication, presentation, and facilitation skills with the ability to advise both technical and executive audiences.

 - Strong interpersonal skills and a proven ability to build trusted client relationships.

 - Exposure to scripting languages such as JavaScript, Python, or PowerShell (preferred).

 - Customer support experience (preferred).

 - Ability to manage competing priorities while maintaining a high standard of quality and customer satisfaction.

 - Brings broad technical expertise and enjoys solving challenging problems through collaboration and innovation.

 

If you believe you could add value to our team but don’t meet all the criteria listed, we still encourage you to apply!



What We Offer

 - Competitive compensation that reflects employees’ skills and contributions.

 - Comprehensive health insurance plans.

 - Wellness programs that promote physical and mental well-being.

 - Traditional and Roth 401(k) savings options with company match.

 - Remote-first with flexible working hours.

 - Prioritized work-life balance, with 15 observed days off in addition to PTO.

 - Family-friendly policies including paid parental leave.

 - Paid life insurance, short-term, and long-term disability insurance.

 - Regular acknowledgement of achievements with recognition programs.

 - Counseling services and resources for personal and professional challenges.

 - Transparent communication about company goals

source_jobs.title: Senior Consultant, Process Improvement
source_jobs.description:
The Senior Consultant, Process Improvement, will translate desired outcomes into successful implemented practices by assisting clients with best practices consideration and the creation of documentation, methodologies and frameworks related to the solution. Perform a variety of assessments to understand client successes and opportunities and create relevant reporting to convey actionable recommendations.

About the Role

 - Manage a group of assigned work requests and business process consulting projects.

 - Identify and recommend improvements to business operations, services, and models centered around IT Service Management (ITSM), Project Portfolio Management (PPM) and AI and automation.

 - Develop process-focused capabilities to increase organizational agility and responsiveness to change.

 - Deliver webinars & workshops on IT Service Management and ITIL-related topics such as Incident Management, Change Management, and Problem Management.

 - Deliver webinars & workshops on Project Portfolio Management topics such as Project Management methodologies (e.g., Waterfall, Agile, Kanban), Project Request Evaluation, and Resource Management.

 - Deliver webinars & workshops on Conversational AI strategy and business process requirements to streamline and automate processes.

 - Perform TeamDynamix Work Management health checks reviewing configuration and usage and providing recommendations.

 - Assist clients with defining and documenting the core processes necessary for successful adoption of the TeamDynamix application.

 - Provide one-on-one, service management, PPM and AI & Automation related coaching to business leaders within the TeamDynamix client base.

 - Act as a solutions architect by guiding and participating in the ongoing development of internal processes. Also act as a subject matter expert for other members of the TeamDynamix Professional Services team.

 - Contribute to sales and marketing activities by contributing to webinars, attending industry conferences/tradeshows and presenting at the annual TeamDynamix Converge.

 - Assist in the creation of best practices training content for course and certification development.

 - Communicate with co-workers, management, clients, and others in a courteous and professional manner.

 - Conform with and abide by all regulations, policies, work procedures and instructions.

About you

 - 4-year college degree

 - Ten or more years of experience working and being a leader on project teams or processes.

 - 5 or more years of experience working and being a leader on service management teams or processes.

 - Ability to define business requirements and translate into creative solutions.

 - Excellent communication and facilitation skills to advise clients.

 - Ability to build and sustain client relationships of trust and respect.

 - Strong presentation and interpersonal skills.

Preferred

 - Master’s degree

 - Industry certifications such as PMP, ITIL, KCS, AI, or operational/process-based methodologies (e.g., Lean).

 - Foundational technical skills to include in-depth understanding of project management and service management software and web-based applications.

 - Previous experience with TDX products.

 - Experience with documenting and improving business processes.

 - Previous exposure or experience to Conversational AI best practices



What We Offer

 - Competitive compensation that reflects employees’ skills and contributions.

 - Comprehensive health insurance plans.

 - Wellness programs that promote physical and mental well-being.

 - Traditional and Roth 401(k) savings options with company match.

 - Remote-first with flexible working hours.

 - Prioritized work-life balance, with 15 observed days off in addition to PTO.

 - Family-friendly policies including paid parental leave.

 - Paid life insurance, short-term, and long-term disability insurance.

 - Regular acknowledgement of achievements with recognition programs.

 - Counseling services and resou

source_jobs.title: Staff Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
As a remote Staff Software Engineer at TeamDynamix, you'll lend your software craftsmanship to our work-management software. We empower customers to do more with fewer resources as they track and manage much of their daily work within the tools created and maintained by you.

We're a multi-tenant SaaS company running on Azure competing with companies more than 10 times our size and we're able to be successful because we hire only the best and brightest. Our small Work Management engineering team of 20 (and growing!) is made up of two smaller full-stack engineering teams plus a quality assurance team to conduct this mission.

In our remote-first environment, every engineer can contribute ideas that get heard and implemented. We believe that technical decisions are best made when we have input from those who are closest to the problem and encourage engineers to think critically about how best to design solutions that satisfy the mission of the team.



The Project You'll Lead: Unified User Management


This isn't a maintenance role. You'll lead the design and delivery of a brand-new User Management System that becomes the single place every TeamDynamix customer goes to manage their users and the access those users have across all of our products.

 

Your job: build a central user database that integrates with every product, syncs users bidirectionally, and gives administrators one clean place to view, link, merge, provision, and audit users across the entire suite.

 

It's a meaty engineering problem with real constraints worth caring about:

 

 - Scale that matters. This system serves our entire customer base, with user records numbering in the tens of millions. Your design decisions around syncing, merging, and querying have to hold up at that volume.

 - Hard distributed-systems work. Omni-directional sync across independently-deployed products — some SaaS-only, some running on-premise — means wrestling with identity reconciliation, conflict resolution, and record linking/merging as a first-class concern, not an afterthought.

 - Built to last and grow. This system is meant to absorb products we build or acquire in the future, so you'll be making architecture decisions that outlive the first release. There are genuinely open questions (authentication and SSO strategy, how activation propagates across products, security requirements) that you'll help answer rather than inherit.

 - API-first and auditable. Every action has to be supported via API with iPaaS connectors on top, and every change has to be captured in a proper before/after audit log. You're building a system other teams and integrations will depend on.

 

Our Tech Stack

Front End

 - ASP.NET http://ASP.NET MVC & WebForms

 - Single Page Applications (SPA) using modern frameworks like Angular and Vue

 - HTML/CSS/JavaScript

 - Bootstrap

 - JQuery/AJAX

Back End

 - Microservices written in .NET 8 running on Kubernetes

 - RESTful WebAPI

 - Current .NET 4.8 Full Framework Web Applications

 - Redis Cache

 - SQL Server

 - Entity Framework

 - SaaS solution hosted in Microsoft Azure

Tooling

 - Visual Studio 2026 & VS Code

 - Azure DevOps for Source Control and CI/CD

 

About You

Senior .NET Software Engineers have a heavy focus on individual contributions to initiatives and are also expected to manage large initiatives from start to finish. In addition to working well with others and being able to plan and execute, you’ll need the following technical skills:

 - 8+ years of software development experience.

 - .NET experience.

 - JavaScript, HTML, and CSS.

 - Experience with SQL Server, T-SQL, stored procedures, and basic database design. We’d prefer a full-stack developer who can work from database to front-end, but you don’t necessarily have to be an SQL guru.
   
   
   
   If you believe you would add value but don't meet all the criteria we've laid out, we'd still love to hear from you.



What We Offer

 - Competitive compensation that reflects

source_jobs.title: Customer Account Executive
source_jobs.description:
About the Role

TeamDynamix is seeking a dedicated, driven Customer Account Executive to join our remote Customer Success team. In this role, you will own and grow a portfolio of existing customer accounts with a strong emphasis on revenue expansion. You will thrive in a fast-paced, high-growth environment, skillfully navigating complex customer needs, negotiating commercial outcomes, and consistently delivering value for both customers and the business. The ideal candidate brings a sales-first mindset, proven ability to generate opportunities within existing accounts, and deep experience in ITSM, ESM, PPM, iPaaS, or related workflow technology solutions.

 - Own and manage a book of existing customers, driving account expansion, and long-term growth.

 - Partner with customers to develop and execute win-win negotiation strategies that maximize contract value while strengthening trusted relationships.

 - Identify and map buying influencers, decision-makers, budgets, and purchasing criteria within assigned accounts.

 - Develop deep expertise in TeamDynamix’s products & licensing models, enabling confident guidance during commercial discussions.

 - Proactively engage customers to uncover needs for consulting, implementation, and professional services.

 - Collaborate closely with Solutions Engineers to lead discovery conversations, position solutions, and deliver product demonstrations for expansion opportunities.

 - Generate and qualify new opportunities within assigned accounts through proactive prospecting and strategic outreach.

 - Maintain accurate pipeline management, forecasting, and CRM hygiene.

About You

 - Highly competitive mindset: You thrive in environments with aggressive growth targets and bring urgency, persistence, and a drive to win.

 - Sales-driven and expansion-focused: You enjoy uncovering whitespace and closing meaningful expansion deals.

 - Personable and credible: You build trust quickly and maintain strong relationships across organizations.

 - Responsive and reliable: You understand that strong follow-up and consistency drive customer confidence.

 - Consultative problem-solver: You ask thoughtful questions, listen actively, and align solutions to real business outcomes.

 - Exceptional team player: You collaborate closely with Sales, Solutions, and Services to deliver a unified customer experience.

 - Persistent and creative in follow-up: You add value at every touchpoint and stay top-of-mind with customers.

About Your Qualifications

 - Associate degree required; bachelor’s degree preferred.

 - 3–5+ years of quota-carrying SaaS sales experience with expansion or account management focus.

 - Highly preferred: Experience in ITSM, ESM, PPM, iPaaS, or related workflow technology markets.

 - Proven track record of driving expansion sales within existing enterprise accounts, particularly within public sector organizations.

 - Experience using Salesforce to manage pipeline, forecast revenue, and track customer engagement.

 - Excellent active listening, critical thinking, and objection-handling skills.

 - Exceptional time management and organizational abilities.

 - Team-oriented with a proven ability to collaborate across sales, solutions, and services teams.

 - Ability to travel multiple times per year.



What We Offer

 - Competitive compensation that reflects employees’ skills and contributions.

 - Comprehensive health insurance plans.

 - Wellness programs that promote physical and mental well-being.

 - Traditional and Roth 401(k) savings options with company match.

 - Remote-first with flexible working hours.

 - Prioritized work-life balance, with 15 observed days off in addition to PTO.

 - Family-friendly policies including paid parental leave.

 - Paid life insurance, short-term, and long-term disability insurance.

 - Regular acknowledgement of achievements with recognition programs.

 - Counseling services and resources for personal and professional challenges.

 - Transpare

source_jobs.title: Integration & Automation Engineer
source_jobs.description:
TeamDynamix is seeking a hands-on Integration & Automation Engineer to join our remote Product organization. In this role you'll design, build, and deliver reusable automations and conversational experiences on TeamDynamix's no-code platform, solving high-value problems for thousands of IT organizations. You'll build on an award-winning iPaaS solution and deliver on a cutting-edge Conversational AI and AI & Automation toolset, helping customers get rapid value from products at the leading edge of the space.

 

What You'll Do:

Build:

 - Design, build, and ship automation flows, integrations, and conversational AI experiences on TeamDynamix iPaaS and CAI.

 - Deliver solutions that tackle novel, high-value problems faced by thousands of IT organizations, expanding what customers can achieve on the platform.

 - Translate product specs and customer requirements into working, scalable automations.

Test, document, and maintain:

 - Own thorough testing and QA before solutions you build hit production.

 - Write clear, easy-to-follow setup guides and configuration documentation that implementation teams and customers can understand.

 - Collaborate with Product to address questions, troubleshooting, and issue resolution.

Required Qualifications:

 - Hands-on experience building on a low-code/no-code automation, integration (iPaaS), or Conversational AI (CAI) platform.

 - Working knowledge of ITSM concepts and workflows (incident, request, change).

 - 2+ years in IT, IT service management, or a related technical role.

 - Clear documentation skills, able to write setup guides that implementation teams and customers can easily follow.

Preferred Qualifications:

 - Hands-on experience building on TeamDynamix iPaaS, Conversational AI (CAI), Work Management, and/or IT Asset Management (ITAM).

 - Comfortable reading API documentation and reasoning about data structures and core programming concepts.

 - Scripting ability in PowerShell, JavaScript, or a similar language.

 - Experience with other iPaaS or automation platforms (Workato, Power Automate, Zapier, Boomi, MuleSoft, Flowgear).

 - ITIL Foundation, HDI, or a related certification.

 - Experience creating reusable solutions, blueprints, or shared configurations that others can deploy or build on.

 - Comfort collaborating directly with product, implementation, or support teams in an iterative, ship-and-improve environment.

 - Baseline understanding of AI capabilities, strengths, and limitations.

 - Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related field.

About You:

 - Comfortable shipping MVP-style and improving iteratively.

 - Detail-oriented about clean configurations, thorough testing, and accurate documentation.

 - A systems thinker who naturally maps inputs, outputs, dependencies, and edge cases.

 - Customer-focused, designing for both the end user and the administrator.

 - Self-directed and quick to absorb new platform features as they ship.



What We Offer

 - Competitive compensation that reflects employees’ skills and contributions.

 - Comprehensive health insurance plans.

 - Wellness programs that promote physical and mental well-being.

 - Traditional and Roth 401(k) savings options with company match.

 - Remote-first with flexible working hours.

 - Prioritized work-life balance, with 15 observed days off in addition to PTO.

 - Family-friendly policies including paid parental leave.

 - Paid life insurance, short-term, and long-term disability insurance.

 - Regular acknowledgement of achievements with recognition programs.

 - Counseling services and resources for personal and professional challenges.

 - Transparent communication about company goals, strategy, and performance.

 - Regular feedback sessions and performance reviews.

 - Anti-bias and anti-harassment training programs.

 - Trust and autonomy in decision-making within your role.

 - Work environment that encourages you to be your authentic self.

 - Flat man

source_jobs.title: Product Manager
source_jobs.description:
TeamDynamix is seeking a strategic, analytical, and customer-focused Product Manager to join our remote Product team. In this role, you will drive the delivery and adoption of high-value product capabilities by transforming customer insights, market research, and business objectives into a clear, actionable product roadmap. You will work closely with customers, engineering, and cross-functional partners to define requirements, prioritize enhancements, and guide product releases. This position is ideal for a product professional who thrives at the intersection of strategy, data, and execution.



About the Role

 - Own and curate product roadmaps that align with customer needs, market opportunities, and business priorities.

 - Gather and analyze product feedback and enhancement requests through direct review, documentation, focus groups, and research.

 - Partner with the product management team to build, validate, and continuously refine the product roadmap.

 - Collaborate with engineering to identify, scope, design, estimate, and deliver product features.

 - Document and analyze enhancement requests and trends to inform product direction.

 - Deliver clear and effective functional product documentation.

 - Plan, coordinate, and manage product version releases.

 - Communicate product plans, updates, and value effectively to customers and internal stakeholders.

 - Assist marketing in communicating product benefits and supporting launch activities.

 - Participate in root cause analysis to recommend product improvements and enhance product fitness.

 - Contribute to business process management initiatives by collecting metrics and improving processes.

 - Conduct research, planning, and integration activities related to mergers and acquisitions.

 - Act as a project manager on select initiatives as needed.

 - Assist in developing product pricing strategies as appropriate.

 - Communicate with colleagues, management, clients, and partners in a professional and courteous manner.

 - Comply with all company regulations, policies, and procedures.



About You

 - Strategic and analytical: You enjoy evaluating customer feedback, usage data, and market trends to inform product decisions.

 - Customer-centered: You are passionate about delivering meaningful value and driving product adoption.

 - Collaborative and influential: You build alignment across teams and effectively navigate trade-offs.

 - Technically fluent: You understand software development concepts, integrations, and APIs.

 - Organized and execution-focused: You balance long-term strategy with day-to-day delivery.

 - Clear communicator: You translate complex ideas into concise, actionable documentation and discussions.



Your Qualifications

 - Bachelor’s degree required.

 - 3–5+ years of experience in product management, business analysis, product marketing, professional services, systems development, or IT operations.

 - Product management and data-driven or AI-driven feature analysis

 - Building integrations with external software systems

 - Integrating multiple products into a unified platform

 - Adding internationalization or translation capabilities

 - Working with user management, authentication, and authorization

 - Competitive research and analysis

Additional preferred, but not required, experience:

 - Experience in IT Operations domains such as ITSM, PPM, Asset Management, or similar

 - Administering or implementing the TeamDynamix Work Management platform



What We Offer

 - Competitive compensation that reflects employees’ skills and contributions.

 - Comprehensive health insurance plans.

 - Wellness programs that promote physical and mental well-being.

 - Traditional and Roth 401(k) savings options with company match.

 - Remote-first with flexible working hours.

 - Prioritized work-life balance, with 15 observed days off in addition to PTO.

 - Family-friendly policies including paid parental leave.

 - Paid life insurance, short-te

source_jobs.title: Enterprise Sales Executive
source_jobs.description:
TeamDynamix is hiring an Enterprise Sales Executive to join our team. We are ideally looking for someone living in California!

TeamDynamix is an award-winning enterprise SaaS solution that brings IT Service Management (ITSM), Enterprise Service Management (ESM) & Project Portfolio Management (PPM) together on one, codeless, simple platform. And we are now introducing Integration Platform-as-a-Service (iPaaS).

As an Enterprise Sales Executive, your primary responsibility is selling the company’s portfolio of SaaS solutions to corporate, higher education, healthcare, and state & local government organizations. Reporting directly to the AVP of Sales, this (remote) position represents a significant opportunity to participate in a high-growth, fast-paced and innovative SaaS organization.

About the role

 - Generate Net-New Logo business through developing relationships and closing business with executive level decision makers.

 - Utilize sound business acumen, strong technical aptitude, and a hunter sales mentality.

 - Serve as a trusted advisor by consistently identifying your customers’ needs and pain-points through effective discovery.

 - Work closely with a team of Sales Development Representatives responsible for supporting you with a robust pipeline of qualified sales opportunities.

 - Partner with Pre-Sales Engineers to communicate and demonstrate the benefits of TeamDynamix’s solutions to decision makers.

 - Meet or exceed quarterly and annual sales objectives and KPIs.

 - Consistently provide reliable sales forecasts.

About you

 - 5+ years of SaaS sales experience in the Enterprise space with a documented history of success.

 - 2+ years of experience in ITSM, or ESM and/or ITAMM.

 - Verifiable and consistent track record of exceeding sales quota, building strong pipelines, and closing business.

 - Must be able to drive end-to-end sales initiatives from pipeline generation to close.

 - Capable of effectively managing a prolonged sales cycle and persuading a committee of key decision makers to act.

 - Skilled in the art of contract negotiation.

 - Excellent written and oral communication skills required.

 - Strong Enterprise level discovery skills and the ability to multi-thread.

 - Strong presentation skills and a polished, professional demeanor.

 - Ability to travel 50% of the time as needed for prospect engagements and industry events.

 - Self-starter and with a relentless work ethic.

 - Preferably located in Central or Pacific time zones.



What We Offer

 - Competitive compensation that reflects employees’ skills and contributions.

 - Comprehensive health insurance plans.

 - Wellness programs that promote physical and mental well-being.

 - Traditional and Roth 401(k) savings options with company match.

 - Remote-first with flexible working hours.

 - Prioritized work-life balance, with 15 observed days off in addition to PTO.

 - Family-friendly policies including paid parental leave.

 - Paid life insurance, short-term, and long-term disability insurance.

 - Regular acknowledgement of achievements with recognition programs.

 - Counseling services and resources for personal and professional challenges.

 - Transparent communication about company goals, strategy, and performance.

 - Regular feedback sessions and performance reviews.

 - Anti-bias and anti-harassment training programs.

 - Trust and autonomy in decision-making within your role.

 - Work environment that encourages you to be your authentic self.

 - Flat management structure with approachable leaders who want to hear your feedback.

 - Backed by two private equity firms that invest in high-growth private companies across North America.
